title: Knowledge Artifact
content:
# Knowledge Artifact

## Introduction

A new GitHub Actions workflow in github.com/manetu/policyengine runs on each MPE release
tag and publishes a single versioned package: `mpe-knowledge-vX.Y.Z`.

### Build steps (run in GH Actions only; not part of the Go build)

1. Walk `docs/docs/`, split each `.md` at H2 boundaries (and at code-fence boundaries
   within an H2 if the section is large), capture
   `{path, heading_path[], frontmatter, text}`.
2. Generate dense embeddings using a small CPU-friendly model (e.g. `bge-small-en-v1.5`,
   384d). The MPE repo is primarily Go, so the build script lives outside the Go module —
   the most pragmatic option is a small Python script (since the ML/embedding ecosystem is
   concentrated there) invoked from the GH Action. No runtime Python on customer infra;
   only GH Actions runs it.
3. Build a lexical index (BM25-style; in-memory, no ES) over the same chunks in the same
   Python step and serialize them. Computing once at artifact build time avoids duplicating
   the work on the consumer side.
4. Hand-curate `curated_baseline.md` (~1–2K tokens). This is the only manually-edited
   file in the artifact; everything else is mechanical from the docs source. Curation
   lives in the OSS repo so it is reviewable alongside doc changes — the same PR that
   changes docs should review the baseline for staleness. Format: plain Markdown,
   single file, no frontmatter required, headings optional. Consumers treat it as raw
   text to prepend to the LLM system prompt.
5. Emit `manifest.json` with:
   ```json
   {
     "manifest_version": 1,
     "mpe_version": "...",
     "embed_model": "...",
     "embed_dims": 768,
     "embed_query_prefix": "search_query: ",
     "chunk_count": ...,
     "build_timestamp": "...",
     "doc_commit_sha": "..."
   }
   ```
   `manifest_version` is the contract version for this artifact schema. Consumers must
   hard-error on an unsupported value so the two repos can evolve at different cadences
   without silent breakage.

### Artifact layout (data-only; no code)

```
mpe-knowledge-vX.Y.Z.jar    # zip containing:
  manifest.json
  curated_baseline.md
  chunks.jsonl              # one JSON object per line
  vectors.bin               # float32 packed, row-major, one row per chunk (embed_dims wide)
  lexical-index.bin         # serialized BM25 postings
  META-INF/maven/com.manetu/mpe-knowledge/pom.xml
```

Estimated size: 60 files × ~5 chunks × (~1 KB text + 1.5 KB vector @384d + lexical
posting) ≈ 30–60 MB uncompressed, ~10–20 MB gzipped. Trivial to embed in a container
image.

The artifact is data-only — no Java/Clojure code. All loading and search logic lives in
the consumer (iamlite backend). This keeps the OSS-repo CI language-agnostic
(Python build → zip → jar) and avoids dragging Clojure tooling into the MPE repo.

## Consumer Contract

The following are the stable, versioned interface that consumers may rely on. Everything
else is an internal implementation detail of `build.py` and may change without notice.

| Resource | Format | Guarantee |
|---|---|---|
| `manifest.json` | JSON, fields as above | All listed fields present; `manifest_version` increments on breaking changes |
| `curated_baseline.md` | Plain UTF-8 Markdown | No frontmatter; safe to prepend verbatim to an LLM system prompt |
| `chunks.jsonl` | One JSON object per line: `{id, path, heading_path[], text, frontmatter}` | `id` is stable for the same doc commit; `heading_path` is an ordered array of heading strings |
| `vectors.bin` | float32, row-major, `chunk_count × embed_dims` | Row `i` corresponds to chunk `i` in `chunks.jsonl` order; `embed_dims` from manifest |
| `lexical-index.bin` | BM25 postings (format documented below) | Term → posting list mapping over chunk ids |

Consumers **must** validate `manifest_version` before loading. Current supported value: `1`.

### BM25 serialization format

All multi-byte integers and floats are **little-endian**. The file is written
deterministically: vocabulary terms are sorted alphabetically; postings within each term
are sorted by ascending `chunk_id`. Tokenization: `chunk["text"].lower().split()`.

**Header — 16 bytes**

| Offset | Size | Type | Value | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------|-------------|
| 0 | 4 | bytes | `BM25` | Magic number (ASCII) |
| 4 | 2 | uint16 LE | `1` | Format version |
| 6 | 4 | float32 LE | e.g. `1.2` | BM25 k1 parameter |
| 10 | 4 | float32 LE | e.g. `0.75` | BM25 b parameter |
| 14 | 2 | bytes | `\x00\x00` | Reserved, must be zero |

**Vocabulary section** (immediately follows header)

| Field | Size | Type | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| `vocab_size` | 4 | uint32 LE | Number of unique terms |
| *(repeated `vocab_size` times)* | | | |
| `term_len` | 2 | uint16 LE | Byte length of the UTF-8 term |
| `term` | `term_len` | UTF-8 bytes | The term string |
| `idf` | 4 | float32 LE | IDF score for this term |

**Postings section** (immediately follows vocabulary; same term order)

| Field | Size | Type | Description |
|-------|------|------|-------------|
| *(repeated `vocab_size` times)* | | | |
| `posting_count` | 4 | uint32 LE | Number of documents containing this term |
| *(repeated `posting_count` times)* | | | |
| `chunk_id` | 4 | uint32 LE | 0-based index into `chunks.jsonl` |
| `tf_score` | 4 | float32 LE | BM25 TF component: `freq*(k1+1) / (freq + k1*(1-b+b*|d|/avgdl))` |

**Scoring**: Full BM25 score for query term `q` in document `d` = `idf[q] * tf_score[q][d]`.

**Validation**: Consumers should assert the first 4 bytes equal `BM25` and `version == 1`
before reading further. Hard-error on unknown versions.

## Publishing

Two channels, one workflow:

1. **GitHub Packages (Maven registry)** — primary. The GH Action uses `actions/setup-java`
   + `mvn deploy` to push `com.manetu:mpe-knowledge:X.Y.Z` to
   `https://maven.pkg.github.com/manetu/policyengine`. Standard Maven coordinates;
   consumers declare `[com.manetu/mpe-knowledge "X.Y.Z"]` in their dependency manager.
2. **GH Releases attachment** — mirror. The same workflow attaches
   `mpe-knowledge-vX.Y.Z.jar` to the existing release on
   https://github.com/manetu/policyengine/releases. Useful for human discoverability,
   ad-hoc download, and as a fallback.

Recommend making the GH Package public (if MPE docs are already public) to avoid requiring
a `read:packages` token on consumer CI and local developer machines.

## Implementation

The build pipeline is designed so a single command produces the artifact identically
whether run by GH Actions or by a developer locally. CI is a thin orchestrator over the
same Makefile targets.

### Files to add

```
knowledge/
  build.py            # chunks, embeds, indexes, packs the jar
  curated_baseline.md # hand-curated MPE essentials
  pom.xml             # Maven coordinates: com.manetu:mpe-knowledge
  requirements.txt    # pinned Python deps (sentence-transformers, rank-bm25, ...)
  Makefile            # knowledge-build, knowledge-install, knowledge-deploy targets

.github/workflows/publish-knowledge.yml
```

### GH Actions workflow

Triggers on release tags. Steps:

1. `actions/checkout`
2. `actions/setup-python` → `make knowledge-build`
3. `actions/setup-java` → `make knowledge-deploy` (mvn deploy to GH Packages)
4. Upload jar as release asset via `actions/upload-release-asset`

CI calls the same `make` targets developers run locally — no duplicated logic.

### Local invocation

| Command | Effect |
|---|---|
| `make knowledge-build` | Creates `knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-<version>.jar`. Sets up a Python venv on first run, installs from `requirements.txt`, runs `build.py`. Version from `git describe` (`vX.Y.Z` on a tag, `vX.Y.Z-<sha>-dirty` otherwise). |
| `make knowledge-install` | Runs `mvn install:install-file` to publish the jar to `~/.m2/repository`. Primary path for day-to-day local development against iamlite. |
| `make knowledge-deploy` | `mvn deploy` to GH Packages. Used by CI; developers normally don't run this. |

### Decision points (confirm during implementation)

- **Embed model:** default suggestion `bge-small-en-v1.5` (384d, ~33 MB, CPU-runnable;
  downloads and caches on first local run).
- **Lexical scorer:** default suggestion BM25 with `k1=1.2, b=0.75` computed via
  `rank-bm25`; serialize to a simple binary layout documented in the Consumer Contract
  section above.
- **GH Package visibility:** public (recommended) or private with token-based auth.

### Determinism

`build.py` should produce byte-identical output (modulo timestamps) for the same doc
commit + same `requirements.txt` lock. Log a sha256 fingerprint of the jar contents
(excluding `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF` timestamp bytes) in CI so drift is observable across
builds.

## Verification

- `make knowledge-build && make knowledge-install` on a clean machine — jar resolves
  locally via `~/.m2`.
- In iamlite, declare `[com.manetu/mpe-knowledge "X.Y.Z-local"]` and confirm `lein deps`
  resolves from `~/.m2`; backend boots and `/api/assistant/info` reports the local version.
- Same git commit on two machines produces matching jar fingerprints.
- `manifest.json` parses and validates: all required fields present, `manifest_version: 1`,
  `chunk_count` matches line count of `chunks.jsonl`, `embed_dims` matches vector row width.
